title: crataegus curvisepala subsp. lindmanii
intro: Crataegus curvisepala subsp. lindmanii, also known as the Lindman's hawthorn, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Lindman's hawthorn include potential cardiovascular support, improved blood circulation, and reduced symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as its potential role in supporting immune function. Lindman's hawthorn is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it can also be consumed as a dried fruit or fruit extract, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal uses.
